text: The procedure waiting for you tomorrow is a small thing measured against everything you've already carried, and you carry it well. Try to remember that the people in that room have done this many quiet times before; your fear is new, but their hands are not. Tonight, let yourself be looked after. Breathe slowly, sleep if you can, and know that I am holding you steadily in my thoughts, on the far side of morning, waiting. You are not walking in alone. You never were.
